1	---
2	date: 2026-04-14T00:00:00+00:00
3	researcher: claude
4	git_commit: b7456d1bbb674ee997b32cfd372c9f99d7032f3f
5	branch: main
6	topic: "Landing page restructure — Linear + Supabase references, isometric graphics, current asset inventory"
7	tags: [research, marketing, landing, www, remotion, isometric, linear-reference, supabase-reference, superseded-in-part]
8	status: complete-but-partially-superseded
9	last_updated: 2026-04-15
10	---
11	
12	# Research: Landing page restructure — Linear + Supabase references, isometric graphics, current asset inventory
13	
14	**Date**: 2026-04-14
15	**Git Commit**: b7456d1bbb674ee997b32cfd372c9f99d7032f3f
16	**Branch**: main
17	
18	> ## ⚠ Strategic Reset (2026-04-15) — read this first
19	>
20	> A follow-up discussion on 2026-04-15 surfaced a fundamentally different positioning for Lightfast that **supersedes most of the section-order proposal in §6 "Proposed section order for the restructured Lightfast landing page"**. The observational research (§1 current inventory, §2 Remotion inventory, §3 extracted Linear SVGs, §4 Linear structural reference, §5 Supabase structural reference, §7 design-system primitives) remains valid and is preserved in place.
21	>
22	> ### What changed
23	>
24	> Lightfast is no longer positioned as a "neural pipeline with integrations" nor as a feature-tour mirror of Linear or Supabase. The new positioning is:
25	>
26	> > **An operating system for product development. For agents and teams.**
27	>
28	> Lightfast is a standalone brand:
29	>
30	> - **Not GitHub-first** — GitHub is not the Foundation, not a hero anchor, not named in the centerpiece diagram. It disappears into an abstract "Code events" input.
31	> - **Not Linear-competitive** — no rebuttal copy, no "Linear is right about X" framing, no named comparisons. Lightfast doesn't position against Linear.
32	> - **Not Claude-Code-first / not terminal-first** — the ICP (cracked engineering teams) is filtered by *tone and what's on the page*, never by explicit "for terminal devs" copy. Claude Code is one destination among several, not a headline.
33	> - **Memory is a component, not the brand** — "Lightfast is a memory layer" was explicitly rejected as positioning. Memory appears as the first of three inner pipeline boxes; the overall product is "an operating system for product development."
34	>
35	> ### The new centerpiece diagram
36	>
37	> Horizontal pipeline (takes *shape* cue from Linear's diagram without adopting Linear's vocabulary):
38	>
39	> ```
40	>                  ┌──────────────── LIGHTFAST ────────────────┐
41	>  Code events ──▶ │  ┌─ Memory ───┐ ┌─ Intelligence ┐ ┌─────┐ │ ──▶ Features
42	>  Signal      ──▶ │  │ Graph      │▶│ Skills        │▶│▓▓░░░│ │ ──▶ Docs
43	>  Feedback    ──▶ │  │ Embeddings │ │ Workflows     │ │Agent│ │ ──▶ Changelog
44	>  Decisions   ──▶ │  │ Context    │ │ Rules         │ │     │ │ ──▶ Customer reply
45	>  Deploys     ──▶ │  │            │ │ Permissions   │ │     │ │ ──▶ Knowledge
46	>                  │  └────────────┘ └───────────────┘ └─────┘ │
47	>                  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘
48	>                  An operating system for product development.
49	> ```
50	>
51	> **Design choices, locked in:**
52	>
53	> - **Three inner boxes, sequential** — Memory → Intelligence → Agents. Not Linear's nested Russian-doll shape.
54	> - **Memory** — first box. Graph, Embeddings, Context. Substrate layer.
55	> - **Intelligence** — middle box. Skills, Workflows, Rules, Permissions. Reasoning/composition layer.
56	> - **Agents** — third box, rendered silently as an iconic gradient bar with no sub-labels. Visual focal point.
57	> - **Inputs** (left column, abstract verbs-of-things): Code events · Signal · Feedback · Decisions · Deploys. No brand names. GitHub is implied, never stated.
58	> - **Outputs** (right column, concrete artifacts): Features · Docs · Changelog · Customer reply · Knowledge. Deliberately specific — the diagram sells the bug→fix→changelog loop before any section copy is read.
59	> - **Brand line under diagram**: *An operating system for product development.*
60	>
61	> ### New page IA (replaces §6 A–M)
62	>
63	> - **Hero** — "An operating system for product development." / "For agents and teams." No GitHub/terminal/Claude-Code mention.
64	> - **§1 Manifesto** — short opinionated prose in Lightfast's own voice. Thesis: *every feature you ship should make your team smarter, not just your product larger.* No competitor references.
65	> - **§2 The diagram** — the pipeline above, annotated.
66	> - **§3 Memory** — Graph, embeddings, context (substrate). v0.1.0 neural pipeline is proof *inside* this section, not a hero feature.
67	> - **§4 Intelligence** — Skills, workflows, rules, permissions (meta-layer). Interview-derived examples: predictive next-step, auto-config, feature→blog/docs/Zendesk drafts.
68	> - **§5 Agents** — Ambient execution. Surfaces (API / SDK / MCP / IDE / CI) shown as destinations, not product chrome.
69	> - **§6 Bug → Fix → Changelog loop** — centerpiece animation; four-frame story demonstrating all three inner boxes in sequence. Beats: (1) signal lands — Sentry + customer report resolve to same symptom; (2) context stitches — correlate to commit, PRs, owner, prior discussion; (3) fix ships — PR opens with context, merges, deploys; (4) loop closes — changelog entry + customer reply + docs update auto-draft, all human-reviewable.
70	> - **§7 Built for teams that ship** — quiet ICP signal through *what's valued* (velocity, compounding knowledge, low ceremony), not *what tools you use*.
71	> - **§8 Changelog + waitlist + footer**
72	>
73	> ### What remains valid from the original research
74	>
75	> - **§1 Current landing inventory** — still accurate; existing components (`HeroChangelogBadge`, `ProductDemo`, `ChangelogPreview`, `WaitlistCTA`, `FAQSection`, `IntegrationShowcase`) continue to have a role, just in different sections of the new IA.
76	> - **§2 Remotion inventory** — still the source of truth for isometric math and visual language. The Memory/Intelligence/Agents box illustrations will likely reuse Remotion primitives as static or animated SVG.
77	> - **§3 Linear SVG assets** — all four extracted SVGs remain valuable reference for visual language (strokes, palette, dashed-line conventions), even though the specific FIG 0.2/0.3/0.4 → Benefits trio mapping and FIG 3.6 → Module 2 Remember mapping are dropped.
78	> - **§4 Linear structural reference** — remains accurate observational research on how Linear builds their pages (numbered modules, FIG callouts, tab-carousels).
79	> - **§5 Supabase structural reference** — remains accurate observational research on Supabase's patterns (product pillars, testimonial wall, open-source anchor).
80	> - **§7 Design-system primitives** — UI primitives, typography (PP Neue Montreal + Geist), OKLCH tokens — all unchanged and still applicable.
81	>
82	> ### What's superseded
83	>
84	> - **§6 Proposed section order (A–M)** — entirely superseded by the new IA above. The Benefits-trio tab-carousel (section D), Module 1 Observe (F), Module 2 Remember (G), and Module 3 Act (H) are all replaced by the Memory/Intelligence/Agents spine + bug→fix→changelog centerpiece.
85	> - **Benefits-trio mapping** — the original mapping of FIG 0.2/0.3/0.4 onto "Built for engineering intelligence / Powered by a neural pipeline / Designed for real-time" is dropped. The Benefits trio no longer exists as a page section.
86	> - **FIG 3.6 → Module 2 Remember** — dropped. FIG 3.6 remains as visual-language reference, but the hexagon-platter motif is not currently mapped to any section in the new IA.
87	> - **"Observe / Remember / Act" pillar vocabulary** — replaced by "Memory / Intelligence / Agents."
88	> - **Open Question 6** (Module 3 Act scope) — no longer applicable; no "Act" pillar exists in the new IA.
89	>
90	> ### Open Questions — disposition
91	>
92	> - **Q1 (Palette direction)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
93	> - **Q2 (Isometric angle 30° vs 54.7°)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
94	> - **Q3 (Illustration delivery: static SVG vs Remotion frame vs animated React SVG)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
95	> - **Q4 (Modal deep-dives acceptable?)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
96	> - **Q5 (Testimonials content availability)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
97	> - **Q6 (Module 3 Act scope)** — no longer applicable (no "Act" pillar)
98	>
99	> ### Next step
100	>
101	> A new research doc will be created to translate this strategic reset into **concrete client-side UI** — actual React components, Remotion adaptations, animation timing, section-by-section design specs, and the four-frame bug→fix→changelog animation storyboard. This current doc is preserved as the historical record of how the direction evolved.
102	>
103	> ---
104	>
105	> **(Original 2026-04-14 research below, preserved for context. Treat §6 as historical; everything else remains current observational research.)**
106	
107	## Research Question
108	
109	The user wants to:
110	1. Extract the isometric SVGs used on Linear's homepage Benefits carousel (three cards — "Built for purpose", "Powered by AI agents", "Designed for speed") and the Git Automations FIG 3.6 hero bento on `/build`, as reference for building similar graphics on Lightfast's landing page.
111	2. Analyse the structure and conversion patterns of `linear.app/` and `supabase.com/` to inform a full restructure of `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx`.
112	3. Do this on a single page (no new routes); surface the features shipped in changelog `v0.1.0` (`apps/www/src/content/changelog/2026-03-26-initial.mdx`).
113	4. Reuse existing isometric work in `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/landing-hero/` where possible.
114	
115	## Summary
116	
117	The current `(landing)/page.tsx` is a 5-section vertical scroll (hero → integrations → product demo → benefits grid → FAQ → changelog preview → CTA). It has a commented-out hero video, no isometric illustration system in React (all isometric work lives in Remotion compositions intended for generated image/video output), no bento/feature-grid primitive, and a flat "6-benefits-as-uniform-cards" treatment that doesn't differentiate product pillars.
118	
119	Linear's homepage is a **numbered-module scroll** (0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5) where each module is a full-height panel with a product UI screenshot on one side and headline + sub-features on the other, plus a three-card benefits **carousel** near the top using isometric figures captioned "FIG 0.2/0.3/0.4". The Git Automations bento on `/build` uses two large stacked isometric hexagons with animated chevron columns between them and a rotating **marquee of feature blocks** on either side.
120	
121	Supabase's homepage is a **product-pillar stack** (Database → Auth → Edge Functions → Storage → Realtime → Vector → Data APIs), each pillar in its own discrete named section, followed by framework logos, customer-story carousel, template gallery, tabbed dashboard showcase, a **Twitter/X testimonial wall**, and an open-source anchor (GitHub star count + "never locked in"). Pricing is hidden from the homepage.
122	
123	The four Linear SVGs have been saved as standalone reference files at `thoughts/shared/research/assets/linear-reference/*.svg`. The existing Remotion compositions already express the visual language Linear uses (isometric tilt 54.7°, dashed grid lines, layered planes, warm near-black palette) — what's missing is a React/SVG equivalent for the marketing page, since Remotion output is intended for generated media, not live DOM.
124	
125	## Detailed Findings
126	
127	### 1. Current Lightfast landing page composition
128	
129	Single file: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx:1-411`.
130	
131	Section order as it stands:
132	1. **Hero** (`page.tsx:223-319`) — full-viewport section. Headline "Building the superintelligence layer for startups" (`page.tsx:277-282`). Single CTA "Join Early Access" (`page.tsx:284-288`). Contains a large 16:9 "Introducing Lightfast — Hello World" blog-post card (`page.tsx:293-312`) and `<HeroChangelogBadge />` at the bottom (`page.tsx:316`). Video hero is commented out (`page.tsx:225-271`).
133	2. **Integrations** (`page.tsx:321-325`) — `<IntegrationShowcase />` logo grid (10 providers).
134	3. **Product Demo** (`page.tsx:327-343`) — bordered, bg-accent section with headline "One place for everything that happens" and `<ProductDemo />` component rendering an interactive mockup.
135	4. **Connect Your Tools / Benefits** (`page.tsx:346-389`) — "Connect Your Tools" pill badge + sub-copy + 6 uniform `lucide-react` icon cards (Activity, Brain, Wand2, Blocks, Plug, Shield). Each card is a bordered div; no illustrations.
136	5. **FAQ** (`page.tsx:392-396`) — `<FAQSection />`.
137	6. **Changelog Preview** (`page.tsx:399-403`) — `<ChangelogPreview />`.
138	7. **CTA** (`page.tsx:407`) — `<WaitlistCTA />`.
139	
140	Reusable components already exist at `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/`:
141	- `hero-changelog-badge.tsx:1-32` — latest changelog link with backdrop blur
142	- `changelog-preview.tsx:1-66` — grid of 4 recent entries
143	- `faq-section.tsx:1-133` — accordion with 9 Q&A pairs, "Join early access" footer CTA
144	- `integration-showcase.tsx:1-35` — 10 provider logo grid
145	- `product-demo.tsx:1-126` — sidebar + event feed + chat empty state mockup
146	- `waitlist-cta.tsx:1-26` — centered CTA section
147	
148	No bento, feature-grid, or isometric-illustration React component exists today.
149	
150	### 2. Existing isometric work (Remotion only)
151	
152	Located at `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/landing-hero/`.
153	
154	`landing-hero.tsx:21-27` — establishes the isometric convention:
155	- Cell 512×512, plane 1536×1536, plane scale 1.1
156	- `ISO_TILT_DEGREES = 54.7356` (standard isometric)
157	- `ISO_ROTATE_Z_DEGREES = -45`
158	- CSS 3D transform: `rotateX(54.7deg) rotateZ(-45deg)`
159	
160	Sections in `sections/`:
161	- `stream-events.tsx:1-280` — animated scrolling event feed with staggered wave entrance, source-coloured icons, expand/collapse rows
162	- `ingested-data.tsx:1-310` — search interface with typewriter query + 3D drop-in result rows
163	- `logo-animation.tsx:1-105` — Lissajous curve trace on an isometric box face with comet trail
164	
165	Shared primitives in `shared/`:
166	- `isometric-card.tsx:1-59` — reusable positioned card (width/height/x/y/entranceFrame)
167	- `grid-lines.tsx:1-157` — dashed iso grid + travelling beam + intersection flash
168	- `iso/` — isometric math utilities (projection, box definitions, face generation)
169	- `timing.ts:1-42` — SMOOTH/GENTLE/SNAPPY spring configs, section timings
170	- `fonts.ts` — PP Neue Montreal + Geist font loading
171	
172	All of this renders via Remotion (video/frame export). None of it ships to the marketing DOM. The math (`shared/iso/*`) is potentially extractable into a framework-agnostic helper, but currently depends on Remotion primitives.
173	
174	Other Remotion compositions at `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/`:
175	- `blog-featured-*` (8 variants: base/concentric/cross/duo/ghost/lissajous/rule/trail) — blog OG image templates
176	- `changelog-v010-featured.tsx`, `changelog-v010-events.tsx` — changelog OG assets
177	- `logo.tsx`, `github-banner.tsx`, `twitter-banner.tsx` — brand asset generators
178	
179	### 3. Linear SVG assets — extracted and preserved
180	
181	Saved to `thoughts/shared/research/assets/linear-reference/`:
182	
183	| File | Source | Dimensions | Purpose |
184	|------|--------|------------|---------|
185	| `01-built-for-purpose.svg` | `linear.app/` FIG 0.2 | 265×262 | Layered-plane motif — single iso card with 5 parallel dashed trace lines radiating below |
186	| `02-powered-by-ai-agents.svg` | `linear.app/` FIG 0.3 | 304×281 | 5-card diamond/pentagon cluster with per-card drop shadow. Companion 3× "grid-dot loader" 16×16 SVGs (upDown / pong / agent keyframed) are animated separately in DOM |
187	| `03-designed-for-speed.svg` | `linear.app/` FIG 0.4 | 272×267 | 15 stacked iso bars forming a momentum pulse wave. Root class `Benefits_momentum` drives animated bar height cycle |
188	| `04-git-automations-fig-3-6.svg` | `linear.app/build` FIG 3.6 | 464×537 | Two stacked isometric hexagonal platters (top = concentric arcs, bottom = octocat silhouette). 3 vertical dashed columns + 2 animated chevron stacks (6 chevrons each, gradient-masked) between them |
189	
190	Shared palette tokens (consistent across all four):
191	- Card fill: `#08090A` (warm near-black)
192	- Bright edge: `#D0D6E0`
193	- Mid stroke: `#62666D`
194	- Dashed stroke: `#3E3E44` with `stroke-dasharray="2 4"` or `"2 6"`
195	- Silhouette stroke: `#8A8F98` at 0.5px
196	- Low-opacity details: 0.4 opacity
197	
198	Animation conventions visible in the source:
199	- Dashed columns use `stroke-dasharray` + class-driven keyframes (`dashUp`, `dashDown`, `dashIsometric`)
200	- Chevron columns are sub-grouped inside `<g mask="url(#...)">` with a vertical gradient mask so chevrons fade in/out at top/bottom of their travel path
201	- Drop shadows use feGaussianBlur `stdDeviation` 6–16, dy 0–12, `flood-opacity` 0.6–1.0
202	- The "grid-dot loader" companions use per-dot classes named `grid-dot-{row}-{col}-{mode}` and presumably CSS keyframes to pulse opacity
203	
204	### 4. Linear landing page structural reference
205	
206	Research source: `https://linear.app/` and `https://linear.app/build`, crawled fresh 2026-04.
207	
208	**Homepage section order (full):**
209	
210	1. **Nav** — sticky. Logo left, product links centre (Intake, Plan, Build, Reviews, Monitor), utility right (Pricing, Enterprise, Contact), CTAs: Open app / Log in / Sign up.
211	2. **Hero** — full viewport. Headline: *"The product development system for teams and agents"*. Sub: *"Purpose-built for planning and building products. Designed for the AI era."* Inline curiosity link: *"Issue tracking is dead — linear.app/next →"*. Ambient-loop product UI vignette (issue card, Slack → issue, Codex agent activity, terminal output).
212	3. **Benefits carousel (tab selector + active panel)** — headline: *"A new species of product tool. Purpose-built for modern teams with AI workflows at its core, Linear sets a new standard for planning and building products."* Three cards (FIG 0.2–0.4): Built for purpose / Powered by AI agents / Designed for speed. Each: isometric illustration top, FIG label, 2-word headline, 1-line sub-copy. Tab-clickable (not scroll-driven).
213	4. **Module 1.0 — Intake** — *"Make product operations self-driving"*. Kanban UI on one side, copy + numbered sub-features (1.1 Linear Agent, 1.2 Triage, 1.3 Customer Requests, 1.4 Linear Asks).
214	5. **Module 2.0 — Plan** — *"Define the product direction"*. Gantt/roadmap UI. Sub-features 2.1–2.4.
215	6. **Module 3.0 — Build** — *"Move work forward across teams and agents"*. Agent command menu + terminal UI. Sub-features 3.1–3.5.
216	7. **Module 4.0 — Diffs** — *"Review PRs and agent output"*. Structural diff UI.
217	8. **Module 5.0 — Monitor** — *"Understand progress at scale"*. Chart + project-status cards.
218	9. **Changelog strip** — 3–4 recent entries with dates, inline horizontal list.
219	10. **Testimonials carousel** — 3 rotating quotes with name/role/company. Auto-rotate.
220	11. **Social proof bar** — *"Linear powers over 25,000 product teams. From ambitious startups to major enterprises."*
221	12. **Bottom CTA** — *"Built for the future. Available today."*
222	
223	**`/build` page layout (single module expanded):**
224	- No separate hero — opens straight into "3.1 Agents" section
225	- "3.3 Linear MCP" — three tool cards (Claude Code / ChatGPT Deep Research / Figma) as horizontal auto-scrolling marquee/ticker
226	- **"3.4 Git automations" (FIG 3.6)** — the bento the user called out:
227	  - Headline: *"Powerful Git automations"* (typographic emphasis split between weights)
228	  - Sub: *"Link Linear with GitHub and GitLab to automate your pull request workflows. Less grunt work, less context switching. More focus, more flow."*
229	  - Central illustration: two stacked iso hexagons connected by animated chevron columns
230	  - **Left and right rails** each show a vertical marquee of 4 feature blocks cycling upward:
231	    - Auto-assign and update — *"Auto-assign issues and move them to a started status when you copy the git branch name"*
232	    - Link and automate PRs — *"Automatically change issue status based on pull/merge request updates"*
233	    - Branch-specific rules — *"Define custom workflow automations based on specific target branches"*
234	    - PR review status — *"See the reviewers and review states of your pull and merge requests directly in Linear"*
235	- "3.5 Cycles" — *"Build momentum with Cycles"* + sub-copy
236	- **Cross-module nav bar** — horizontal strip linking back to 1.0 / 2.0 / 4.0 / 5.0 with each module's one-line description + "Learn more"
237	- Shared footer: Testimonials / 25K teams bar / "Built for the future. Available today." CTA
238	
239	**Visual language (high confidence):**
240	- Background: warm near-black (`#0F0E0D` range, post-March-2026 refresh moved away from cool-blue dark)
241	- Cards: ~1–2 lightness steps above bg, soft low-opacity warm-gray borders
242	- Radius: 8–12px cards, rounded tabs, 6px buttons
243	- Typography: geometric sans for display (48–72px, weight 600–700), 24–32px medium for sub-headlines, 14–16px regular body at ~70% opacity
244	- **FIG labels**: small uppercase monospace-weight text, loose (no box) at bottom-left/right of illustration, reads like engineering schematic callouts
245	- Container max-width: ~1200–1280px
246	- Section padding: ~80–120px vertical; feature modules approach 100vh
247	
248	**Isometric illustration conventions:**
249	- Standard 30° isometric (different from Lightfast's 54.7° Remotion tilt — Linear's is shallower)
250	- Thin strokes (1–1.5px equivalent at display size)
251	- **Dashed trace line motif** — recurring. No arrowheads; direction implied by animation or grouping
252	- **Layered stacked-plane motif** — always bottom-left → top-right in iso space
253	- Subtle drop shadows (soft, not dramatic)
254	- Accent highlights in purple-to-blue gradient range (brand)
255	- Ambient loops for in-card illustrations (always running); scroll-triggered reveals for full product UI sections
256	
257	**Conversion elements:**
258	- Primary CTA: "Sign up" (nav persistent) + "Get started" (bottom)
259	- Secondary: "Contact sales", "Open app"
260	- No pricing on homepage — pricing is a separate route
261	- Testimonials AFTER features, before bottom CTA
262	- Social proof bar is a single headline with a specific number
263	
264	### 5. Supabase landing page structural reference
265	
266	Research source: `https://supabase.com/`, crawled fresh 2026-04.
267	
268	**Section order:**
269	1. **Announcement banner** — one-line bar above nav (e.g. *"State of Startups 2026: Take the survey"*)
270	2. **Hero** — headline: *"Build in a weekend / Scale to millions"* (two-line display). Sub: *"Supabase is the Postgres development platform. Start your project with a Postgres database, Authentication, instant APIs, Edge Functions, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Vector embeddings."* CTAs: "Start your project" (green filled) + "Request a demo" (ghost)
271	3. **Logo wall** — eyebrow: *"Trusted by fast-growing companies worldwide."* Horizontal strip, no stats
272	4. **Product pillars (sequential, one per section)** — Postgres Database / Authentication / Edge Functions / Storage / Realtime / Vector / Data APIs. Each pillar: named section with headline, sub-copy, 2–3 feature tags, optional inline code (e.g. `$ supabase functions deploy`). Mix of bento cards and split-layout panels.
273	5. **Framework logos row** — *"Use Supabase with any framework."*
274	6. **Customer stories carousel** — three outcome-first headlines rotating (e.g. *"Maergo's Express Delivery: How Supabase Helped Achieve Scalability, Speed, and Cost Saving"*). CTAs: View all stories / View events.
275	7. **Template gallery** — *"Start building in seconds."* Card grid of community starters.
276	8. **Tabbed dashboard showcase** — *"Stay productive and manage your app without leaving the dashboard."* Tabs: Table Editor / SQL Editor / RLS Policies.
277	9. **Community testimonial wall** — *"Join the community."* Masonry/grid of raw Twitter/X post cards (14+), verbatim, with handles. Auto-cycling. CTA: Join us on Discord.
278	10. **Open-source anchor** — *"Open source from day one."* Copy: *"Read, contribute, self-host. You're never locked in, and always in control."* GitHub badge: `@supabase 100.8K — Top 100 GitHub repos`.
279	11. **Final CTA** — mirrors hero exactly: *"Build in a weekend, scale to millions."* Same two CTAs.
280	12. Footer.
281	
282	**Visual language:**
283	- Background: `#1C1C1C` (primary) / `#171717` (alt); card `#232323`
284	- Single brand accent: green `#3ECF8E` — applied sparingly (primary CTA, SQL keywords in code blocks, GitHub star number)
285	- Text: `#EDEDED` primary / `#A1A1A1` secondary; borders at 6% white default / 12% strong
286	- Typography: Circular sans (display ~56px at `-0.02em`), Source Code Pro mono (13px at 1.7 line-height for code)
287	- Radius: 6px buttons, 8px cards, 4px inputs
288	- Code blocks: 24px padding, `#171717` bg, 1px border at 6% white, syntax highlighting maps green to keywords
289	
290	**Conversion signals:**
291	- No pricing numbers on homepage
292	- Free-tier signal via GitHub stars (100.8K) + "never locked in" copy
293	- Twitter testimonials (unpolished, with handles) replace enterprise logo wall as primary social proof
294	- Developer-identity positioning: velocity promise ("weekend"), code as first-class UI, open source as last objection cleared before final CTA
295	
296	### 6. Proposed section order for the restructured Lightfast landing page
297	
298	> **⚠ SUPERSEDED (2026-04-15).** The A–M section order below is no longer the active plan. See the Strategic Reset banner at the top of this document for the replacement IA (Hero → Manifesto → Pipeline diagram → Memory → Intelligence → Agents → Bug→Fix→Changelog loop → Built for teams that ship → Changelog/waitlist/footer). The observational research in §1–§5 and §7 remains valid; only this mapping is deprecated.
299	
300	This is a documentation synthesis mapping existing assets + new isometric sections to a single-page structure. **Not a decided plan** — open questions listed after.

# Research: Landing page restructure — Linear + Supabase references, isometric graphics, current asset inventory

**Date**: 2026-04-14
**Git Commit**: b7456d1bbb674ee997b32cfd372c9f99d7032f3f
**Branch**: main

> ## ⚠ Strategic Reset (2026-04-15) — read this first
>
> A follow-up discussion on 2026-04-15 surfaced a fundamentally different positioning for Lightfast that **supersedes most of the section-order proposal in §6 "Proposed section order for the restructured Lightfast landing page"**. The observational research (§1 current inventory, §2 Remotion inventory, §3 extracted Linear SVGs, §4 Linear structural reference, §5 Supabase structural reference, §7 design-system primitives) remains valid and is preserved in place.
>
> ### What changed
>
> Lightfast is no longer positioned as a "neural pipeline with integrations" nor as a feature-tour mirror of Linear or Supabase. The new positioning is:
>
> > **An operating system for product development. For agents and teams.**
>
> Lightfast is a standalone brand:
>
> - **Not GitHub-first** — GitHub is not the Foundation, not a hero anchor, not named in the centerpiece diagram. It disappears into an abstract "Code events" input.
> - **Not Linear-competitive** — no rebuttal copy, no "Linear is right about X" framing, no named comparisons. Lightfast doesn't position against Linear.
> - **Not Claude-Code-first / not terminal-first** — the ICP (cracked engineering teams) is filtered by *tone and what's on the page*, never by explicit "for terminal devs" copy. Claude Code is one destination among several, not a headline.
> - **Memory is a component, not the brand** — "Lightfast is a memory layer" was explicitly rejected as positioning. Memory appears as the first of three inner pipeline boxes; the overall product is "an operating system for product development."
>
> ### The new centerpiece diagram
>
> Horizontal pipeline (takes *shape* cue from Linear's diagram without adopting Linear's vocabulary):
>
> ```
>                  ┌──────────────── LIGHTFAST ────────────────┐
>  Code events ──▶ │  ┌─ Memory ───┐ ┌─ Intelligence ┐ ┌─────┐ │ ──▶ Features
>  Signal      ──▶ │  │ Graph      │▶│ Skills        │▶│▓▓░░░│ │ ──▶ Docs
>  Feedback    ──▶ │  │ Embeddings │ │ Workflows     │ │Agent│ │ ──▶ Changelog
>  Decisions   ──▶ │  │ Context    │ │ Rules         │ │     │ │ ──▶ Customer reply
>  Deploys     ──▶ │  │            │ │ Permissions   │ │     │ │ ──▶ Knowledge
>                  │  └────────────┘ └───────────────┘ └─────┘ │
>                  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘
>                  An operating system for product development.
> ```
>
> **Design choices, locked in:**
>
> - **Three inner boxes, sequential** — Memory → Intelligence → Agents. Not Linear's nested Russian-doll shape.
> - **Memory** — first box. Graph, Embeddings, Context. Substrate layer.
> - **Intelligence** — middle box. Skills, Workflows, Rules, Permissions. Reasoning/composition layer.
> - **Agents** — third box, rendered silently as an iconic gradient bar with no sub-labels. Visual focal point.
> - **Inputs** (left column, abstract verbs-of-things): Code events · Signal · Feedback · Decisions · Deploys. No brand names. GitHub is implied, never stated.
> - **Outputs** (right column, concrete artifacts): Features · Docs · Changelog · Customer reply · Knowledge. Deliberately specific — the diagram sells the bug→fix→changelog loop before any section copy is read.
> - **Brand line under diagram**: *An operating system for product development.*
>
> ### New page IA (replaces §6 A–M)
>
> - **Hero** — "An operating system for product development." / "For agents and teams." No GitHub/terminal/Claude-Code mention.
> - **§1 Manifesto** — short opinionated prose in Lightfast's own voice. Thesis: *every feature you ship should make your team smarter, not just your product larger.* No competitor references.
> - **§2 The diagram** — the pipeline above, annotated.
> - **§3 Memory** — Graph, embeddings, context (substrate). v0.1.0 neural pipeline is proof *inside* this section, not a hero feature.
> - **§4 Intelligence** — Skills, workflows, rules, permissions (meta-layer). Interview-derived examples: predictive next-step, auto-config, feature→blog/docs/Zendesk drafts.
> - **§5 Agents** — Ambient execution. Surfaces (API / SDK / MCP / IDE / CI) shown as destinations, not product chrome.
> - **§6 Bug → Fix → Changelog loop** — centerpiece animation; four-frame story demonstrating all three inner boxes in sequence. Beats: (1) signal lands — Sentry + customer report resolve to same symptom; (2) context stitches — correlate to commit, PRs, owner, prior discussion; (3) fix ships — PR opens with context, merges, deploys; (4) loop closes — changelog entry + customer reply + docs update auto-draft, all human-reviewable.
> - **§7 Built for teams that ship** — quiet ICP signal through *what's valued* (velocity, compounding knowledge, low ceremony), not *what tools you use*.
> - **§8 Changelog + waitlist + footer**
>
> ### What remains valid from the original research
>
> - **§1 Current landing inventory** — still accurate; existing components (`HeroChangelogBadge`, `ProductDemo`, `ChangelogPreview`, `WaitlistCTA`, `FAQSection`, `IntegrationShowcase`) continue to have a role, just in different sections of the new IA.
> - **§2 Remotion inventory** — still the source of truth for isometric math and visual language. The Memory/Intelligence/Agents box illustrations will likely reuse Remotion primitives as static or animated SVG.
> - **§3 Linear SVG assets** — all four extracted SVGs remain valuable reference for visual language (strokes, palette, dashed-line conventions), even though the specific FIG 0.2/0.3/0.4 → Benefits trio mapping and FIG 3.6 → Module 2 Remember mapping are dropped.
> - **§4 Linear structural reference** — remains accurate observational research on how Linear builds their pages (numbered modules, FIG callouts, tab-carousels).
> - **§5 Supabase structural reference** — remains accurate observational research on Supabase's patterns (product pillars, testimonial wall, open-source anchor).
> - **§7 Design-system primitives** — UI primitives, typography (PP Neue Montreal + Geist), OKLCH tokens — all unchanged and still applicable.
>
> ### What's superseded
>
> - **§6 Proposed section order (A–M)** — entirely superseded by the new IA above. The Benefits-trio tab-carousel (section D), Module 1 Observe (F), Module 2 Remember (G), and Module 3 Act (H) are all replaced by the Memory/Intelligence/Agents spine + bug→fix→changelog centerpiece.
> - **Benefits-trio mapping** — the original mapping of FIG 0.2/0.3/0.4 onto "Built for engineering intelligence / Powered by a neural pipeline / Designed for real-time" is dropped. The Benefits trio no longer exists as a page section.
> - **FIG 3.6 → Module 2 Remember** — dropped. FIG 3.6 remains as visual-language reference, but the hexagon-platter motif is not currently mapped to any section in the new IA.
> - **"Observe / Remember / Act" pillar vocabulary** — replaced by "Memory / Intelligence / Agents."
> - **Open Question 6** (Module 3 Act scope) — no longer applicable; no "Act" pillar exists in the new IA.
>
> ### Open Questions — disposition
>
> - **Q1 (Palette direction)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
> - **Q2 (Isometric angle 30° vs 54.7°)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
> - **Q3 (Illustration delivery: static SVG vs Remotion frame vs animated React SVG)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
> - **Q4 (Modal deep-dives acceptable?)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
> - **Q5 (Testimonials content availability)** — still open; defer to follow-up research pass
> - **Q6 (Module 3 Act scope)** — no longer applicable (no "Act" pillar)
>
> ### Next step
>
> A new research doc will be created to translate this strategic reset into **concrete client-side UI** — actual React components, Remotion adaptations, animation timing, section-by-section design specs, and the four-frame bug→fix→changelog animation storyboard. This current doc is preserved as the historical record of how the direction evolved.
>
> ---
>
> **(Original 2026-04-14 research below, preserved for context. Treat §6 as historical; everything else remains current observational research.)**

## Research Question

The user wants to:
1. Extract the isometric SVGs used on Linear's homepage Benefits carousel (three cards — "Built for purpose", "Powered by AI agents", "Designed for speed") and the Git Automations FIG 3.6 hero bento on `/build`, as reference for building similar graphics on Lightfast's landing page.
2. Analyse the structure and conversion patterns of `linear.app/` and `supabase.com/` to inform a full restructure of `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx`.
3. Do this on a single page (no new routes); surface the features shipped in changelog `v0.1.0` (`apps/www/src/content/changelog/2026-03-26-initial.mdx`).
4. Reuse existing isometric work in `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/landing-hero/` where possible.

## Summary

The current `(landing)/page.tsx` is a 5-section vertical scroll (hero → integrations → product demo → benefits grid → FAQ → changelog preview → CTA). It has a commented-out hero video, no isometric illustration system in React (all isometric work lives in Remotion compositions intended for generated image/video output), no bento/feature-grid primitive, and a flat "6-benefits-as-uniform-cards" treatment that doesn't differentiate product pillars.

Linear's homepage is a **numbered-module scroll** (0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5) where each module is a full-height panel with a product UI screenshot on one side and headline + sub-features on the other, plus a three-card benefits **carousel** near the top using isometric figures captioned "FIG 0.2/0.3/0.4". The Git Automations bento on `/build` uses two large stacked isometric hexagons with animated chevron columns between them and a rotating **marquee of feature blocks** on either side.

Supabase's homepage is a **product-pillar stack** (Database → Auth → Edge Functions → Storage → Realtime → Vector → Data APIs), each pillar in its own discrete named section, followed by framework logos, customer-story carousel, template gallery, tabbed dashboard showcase, a **Twitter/X testimonial wall**, and an open-source anchor (GitHub star count + "never locked in"). Pricing is hidden from the homepage.

The four Linear SVGs have been saved as standalone reference files at `thoughts/shared/research/assets/linear-reference/*.svg`. The existing Remotion compositions already express the visual language Linear uses (isometric tilt 54.7°, dashed grid lines, layered planes, warm near-black palette) — what's missing is a React/SVG equivalent for the marketing page, since Remotion output is intended for generated media, not live DOM.

## Detailed Findings

### 1. Current Lightfast landing page composition

Single file: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx:1-411`.

Section order as it stands:
1. **Hero** (`page.tsx:223-319`) — full-viewport section. Headline "Building the superintelligence layer for startups" (`page.tsx:277-282`). Single CTA "Join Early Access" (`page.tsx:284-288`). Contains a large 16:9 "Introducing Lightfast — Hello World" blog-post card (`page.tsx:293-312`) and `<HeroChangelogBadge />` at the bottom (`page.tsx:316`). Video hero is commented out (`page.tsx:225-271`).
2. **Integrations** (`page.tsx:321-325`) — `<IntegrationShowcase />` logo grid (10 providers).
3. **Product Demo** (`page.tsx:327-343`) — bordered, bg-accent section with headline "One place for everything that happens" and `<ProductDemo />` component rendering an interactive mockup.
4. **Connect Your Tools / Benefits** (`page.tsx:346-389`) — "Connect Your Tools" pill badge + sub-copy + 6 uniform `lucide-react` icon cards (Activity, Brain, Wand2, Blocks, Plug, Shield). Each card is a bordered div; no illustrations.
5. **FAQ** (`page.tsx:392-396`) — `<FAQSection />`.
6. **Changelog Preview** (`page.tsx:399-403`) — `<ChangelogPreview />`.
7. **CTA** (`page.tsx:407`) — `<WaitlistCTA />`.

Reusable components already exist at `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/`:
- `hero-changelog-badge.tsx:1-32` — latest changelog link with backdrop blur
- `changelog-preview.tsx:1-66` — grid of 4 recent entries
- `faq-section.tsx:1-133` — accordion with 9 Q&A pairs, "Join early access" footer CTA
- `integration-showcase.tsx:1-35` — 10 provider logo grid
- `product-demo.tsx:1-126` — sidebar + event feed + chat empty state mockup
- `waitlist-cta.tsx:1-26` — centered CTA section

No bento, feature-grid, or isometric-illustration React component exists today.

### 2. Existing isometric work (Remotion only)

Located at `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/landing-hero/`.

`landing-hero.tsx:21-27` — establishes the isometric convention:
- Cell 512×512, plane 1536×1536, plane scale 1.1
- `ISO_TILT_DEGREES = 54.7356` (standard isometric)
- `ISO_ROTATE_Z_DEGREES = -45`
- CSS 3D transform: `rotateX(54.7deg) rotateZ(-45deg)`

Sections in `sections/`:
- `stream-events.tsx:1-280` — animated scrolling event feed with staggered wave entrance, source-coloured icons, expand/collapse rows
- `ingested-data.tsx:1-310` — search interface with typewriter query + 3D drop-in result rows
- `logo-animation.tsx:1-105` — Lissajous curve trace on an isometric box face with comet trail

Shared primitives in `shared/`:
- `isometric-card.tsx:1-59` — reusable positioned card (width/height/x/y/entranceFrame)
- `grid-lines.tsx:1-157` — dashed iso grid + travelling beam + intersection flash
- `iso/` — isometric math utilities (projection, box definitions, face generation)
- `timing.ts:1-42` — SMOOTH/GENTLE/SNAPPY spring configs, section timings
- `fonts.ts` — PP Neue Montreal + Geist font loading

All of this renders via Remotion (video/frame export). None of it ships to the marketing DOM. The math (`shared/iso/*`) is potentially extractable into a framework-agnostic helper, but currently depends on Remotion primitives.

Other Remotion compositions at `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/`:
- `blog-featured-*` (8 variants: base/concentric/cross/duo/ghost/lissajous/rule/trail) — blog OG image templates
- `changelog-v010-featured.tsx`, `changelog-v010-events.tsx` — changelog OG assets
- `logo.tsx`, `github-banner.tsx`, `twitter-banner.tsx` — brand asset generators

### 3. Linear SVG assets — extracted and preserved

Saved to `thoughts/shared/research/assets/linear-reference/`:

| File | Source | Dimensions | Purpose |
|------|--------|------------|---------|
| `01-built-for-purpose.svg` | `linear.app/` FIG 0.2 | 265×262 | Layered-plane motif — single iso card with 5 parallel dashed trace lines radiating below |
| `02-powered-by-ai-agents.svg` | `linear.app/` FIG 0.3 | 304×281 | 5-card diamond/pentagon cluster with per-card drop shadow. Companion 3× "grid-dot loader" 16×16 SVGs (upDown / pong / agent keyframed) are animated separately in DOM |
| `03-designed-for-speed.svg` | `linear.app/` FIG 0.4 | 272×267 | 15 stacked iso bars forming a momentum pulse wave. Root class `Benefits_momentum` drives animated bar height cycle |
| `04-git-automations-fig-3-6.svg` | `linear.app/build` FIG 3.6 | 464×537 | Two stacked isometric hexagonal platters (top = concentric arcs, bottom = octocat silhouette). 3 vertical dashed columns + 2 animated chevron stacks (6 chevrons each, gradient-masked) between them |

Shared palette tokens (consistent across all four):
- Card fill: `#08090A` (warm near-black)
- Bright edge: `#D0D6E0`
- Mid stroke: `#62666D`
- Dashed stroke: `#3E3E44` with `stroke-dasharray="2 4"` or `"2 6"`
- Silhouette stroke: `#8A8F98` at 0.5px
- Low-opacity details: 0.4 opacity

Animation conventions visible in the source:
- Dashed columns use `stroke-dasharray` + class-driven keyframes (`dashUp`, `dashDown`, `dashIsometric`)
- Chevron columns are sub-grouped inside `<g mask="url(#...)">` with a vertical gradient mask so chevrons fade in/out at top/bottom of their travel path
- Drop shadows use feGaussianBlur `stdDeviation` 6–16, dy 0–12, `flood-opacity` 0.6–1.0
- The "grid-dot loader" companions use per-dot classes named `grid-dot-{row}-{col}-{mode}` and presumably CSS keyframes to pulse opacity

### 4. Linear landing page structural reference

Research source: `https://linear.app/` and `https://linear.app/build`, crawled fresh 2026-04.

**Homepage section order (full):**

1. **Nav** — sticky. Logo left, product links centre (Intake, Plan, Build, Reviews, Monitor), utility right (Pricing, Enterprise, Contact), CTAs: Open app / Log in / Sign up.
2. **Hero** — full viewport. Headline: *"The product development system for teams and agents"*. Sub: *"Purpose-built for planning and building products. Designed for the AI era."* Inline curiosity link: *"Issue tracking is dead — linear.app/next →"*. Ambient-loop product UI vignette (issue card, Slack → issue, Codex agent activity, terminal output).
3. **Benefits carousel (tab selector + active panel)** — headline: *"A new species of product tool. Purpose-built for modern teams with AI workflows at its core, Linear sets a new standard for planning and building products."* Three cards (FIG 0.2–0.4): Built for purpose / Powered by AI agents / Designed for speed. Each: isometric illustration top, FIG label, 2-word headline, 1-line sub-copy. Tab-clickable (not scroll-driven).
4. **Module 1.0 — Intake** — *"Make product operations self-driving"*. Kanban UI on one side, copy + numbered sub-features (1.1 Linear Agent, 1.2 Triage, 1.3 Customer Requests, 1.4 Linear Asks).
5. **Module 2.0 — Plan** — *"Define the product direction"*. Gantt/roadmap UI. Sub-features 2.1–2.4.
6. **Module 3.0 — Build** — *"Move work forward across teams and agents"*. Agent command menu + terminal UI. Sub-features 3.1–3.5.
7. **Module 4.0 — Diffs** — *"Review PRs and agent output"*. Structural diff UI.
8. **Module 5.0 — Monitor** — *"Understand progress at scale"*. Chart + project-status cards.
9. **Changelog strip** — 3–4 recent entries with dates, inline horizontal list.
10. **Testimonials carousel** — 3 rotating quotes with name/role/company. Auto-rotate.
11. **Social proof bar** — *"Linear powers over 25,000 product teams. From ambitious startups to major enterprises."*
12. **Bottom CTA** — *"Built for the future. Available today."*

**`/build` page layout (single module expanded):**
- No separate hero — opens straight into "3.1 Agents" section
- "3.3 Linear MCP" — three tool cards (Claude Code / ChatGPT Deep Research / Figma) as horizontal auto-scrolling marquee/ticker
- **"3.4 Git automations" (FIG 3.6)** — the bento the user called out:
  - Headline: *"Powerful Git automations"* (typographic emphasis split between weights)
  - Sub: *"Link Linear with GitHub and GitLab to automate your pull request workflows. Less grunt work, less context switching. More focus, more flow."*
  - Central illustration: two stacked iso hexagons connected by animated chevron columns
  - **Left and right rails** each show a vertical marquee of 4 feature blocks cycling upward:
    - Auto-assign and update — *"Auto-assign issues and move them to a started status when you copy the git branch name"*
    - Link and automate PRs — *"Automatically change issue status based on pull/merge request updates"*
    - Branch-specific rules — *"Define custom workflow automations based on specific target branches"*
    - PR review status — *"See the reviewers and review states of your pull and merge requests directly in Linear"*
- "3.5 Cycles" — *"Build momentum with Cycles"* + sub-copy
- **Cross-module nav bar** — horizontal strip linking back to 1.0 / 2.0 / 4.0 / 5.0 with each module's one-line description + "Learn more"
- Shared footer: Testimonials / 25K teams bar / "Built for the future. Available today." CTA

**Visual language (high confidence):**
- Background: warm near-black (`#0F0E0D` range, post-March-2026 refresh moved away from cool-blue dark)
- Cards: ~1–2 lightness steps above bg, soft low-opacity warm-gray borders
- Radius: 8–12px cards, rounded tabs, 6px buttons
- Typography: geometric sans for display (48–72px, weight 600–700), 24–32px medium for sub-headlines, 14–16px regular body at ~70% opacity
- **FIG labels**: small uppercase monospace-weight text, loose (no box) at bottom-left/right of illustration, reads like engineering schematic callouts
- Container max-width: ~1200–1280px
- Section padding: ~80–120px vertical; feature modules approach 100vh

**Isometric illustration conventions:**
- Standard 30° isometric (different from Lightfast's 54.7° Remotion tilt — Linear's is shallower)
- Thin strokes (1–1.5px equivalent at display size)
- **Dashed trace line motif** — recurring. No arrowheads; direction implied by animation or grouping
- **Layered stacked-plane motif** — always bottom-left → top-right in iso space
- Subtle drop shadows (soft, not dramatic)
- Accent highlights in purple-to-blue gradient range (brand)
- Ambient loops for in-card illustrations (always running); scroll-triggered reveals for full product UI sections

**Conversion elements:**
- Primary CTA: "Sign up" (nav persistent) + "Get started" (bottom)
- Secondary: "Contact sales", "Open app"
- No pricing on homepage — pricing is a separate route
- Testimonials AFTER features, before bottom CTA
- Social proof bar is a single headline with a specific number

### 5. Supabase landing page structural reference

Research source: `https://supabase.com/`, crawled fresh 2026-04.

**Section order:**
1. **Announcement banner** — one-line bar above nav (e.g. *"State of Startups 2026: Take the survey"*)
2. **Hero** — headline: *"Build in a weekend / Scale to millions"* (two-line display). Sub: *"Supabase is the Postgres development platform. Start your project with a Postgres database, Authentication, instant APIs, Edge Functions, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Vector embeddings."* CTAs: "Start your project" (green filled) + "Request a demo" (ghost)
3. **Logo wall** — eyebrow: *"Trusted by fast-growing companies worldwide."* Horizontal strip, no stats
4. **Product pillars (sequential, one per section)** — Postgres Database / Authentication / Edge Functions / Storage / Realtime / Vector / Data APIs. Each pillar: named section with headline, sub-copy, 2–3 feature tags, optional inline code (e.g. `$ supabase functions deploy`). Mix of bento cards and split-layout panels.
5. **Framework logos row** — *"Use Supabase with any framework."*
6. **Customer stories carousel** — three outcome-first headlines rotating (e.g. *"Maergo's Express Delivery: How Supabase Helped Achieve Scalability, Speed, and Cost Saving"*). CTAs: View all stories / View events.
7. **Template gallery** — *"Start building in seconds."* Card grid of community starters.
8. **Tabbed dashboard showcase** — *"Stay productive and manage your app without leaving the dashboard."* Tabs: Table Editor / SQL Editor / RLS Policies.
9. **Community testimonial wall** — *"Join the community."* Masonry/grid of raw Twitter/X post cards (14+), verbatim, with handles. Auto-cycling. CTA: Join us on Discord.
10. **Open-source anchor** — *"Open source from day one."* Copy: *"Read, contribute, self-host. You're never locked in, and always in control."* GitHub badge: `@supabase 100.8K — Top 100 GitHub repos`.
11. **Final CTA** — mirrors hero exactly: *"Build in a weekend, scale to millions."* Same two CTAs.
12. Footer.

**Visual language:**
- Background: `#1C1C1C` (primary) / `#171717` (alt); card `#232323`
- Single brand accent: green `#3ECF8E` — applied sparingly (primary CTA, SQL keywords in code blocks, GitHub star number)
- Text: `#EDEDED` primary / `#A1A1A1` secondary; borders at 6% white default / 12% strong
- Typography: Circular sans (display ~56px at `-0.02em`), Source Code Pro mono (13px at 1.7 line-height for code)
- Radius: 6px buttons, 8px cards, 4px inputs
- Code blocks: 24px padding, `#171717` bg, 1px border at 6% white, syntax highlighting maps green to keywords

**Conversion signals:**
- No pricing numbers on homepage
- Free-tier signal via GitHub stars (100.8K) + "never locked in" copy
- Twitter testimonials (unpolished, with handles) replace enterprise logo wall as primary social proof
- Developer-identity positioning: velocity promise ("weekend"), code as first-class UI, open source as last objection cleared before final CTA

### 6. Proposed section order for the restructured Lightfast landing page

> **⚠ SUPERSEDED (2026-04-15).** The A–M section order below is no longer the active plan. See the Strategic Reset banner at the top of this document for the replacement IA (Hero → Manifesto → Pipeline diagram → Memory → Intelligence → Agents → Bug→Fix→Changelog loop → Built for teams that ship → Changelog/waitlist/footer). The observational research in §1–§5 and §7 remains valid; only this mapping is deprecated.

This is a documentation synthesis mapping existing assets + new isometric sections to a single-page structure. **Not a decided plan** — open questions listed after.

4 5 # Lightfast 6 7 [![License: FSL-1.1-ALv2](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-FSL--1.1--ALv2-orange.svg)](LICENSE) 8 [![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/lightfast.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/lightfast) 9 [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.0+-blue.svg)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) 10 [![CI Status](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 11 [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/lightfastai/lightfast)](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/stargazers) 12 13 **An operating system for product development.** 14 **For agents and teams.** 15 16 [Website](https://lightfast.ai) · [Documentation](https://lightfast.ai/docs/get-started/overview) · [Discord](https://discord.gg/YqPDfcar2C) 17 18 Lightfast ingests events from your stack — code, signals, feedback, decisions, deploys — and stores them as cited memory that agents and engineers query through the same primitives. The operating layer is a pipeline: **memory → intelligence → agents**. `v0.1.0` ships memory and a proxy for agent actions; intelligence, and the auto-drafted artifacts that close the loop, are in development. 19 20 ## Memory 21 22 Memory is the substrate, and the layer Lightfast ships today. 23 24 Every event from every connected tool flows into a temporal graph with semantic embeddings, cited by source. Not a log — a graph that resolves commits, PRs, deployments, incidents, and the people behind them into entities and traces the relationships between them. `Explore` answers questions against that graph with streaming, cited responses grounded in the actual events. 25 26 The direction: causal reasoning across tools — not just storing that *a PR merged* and *an error spiked*, but understanding which commit caused which incident, which ticket tracks the fix, and which deployment shipped it. The longer Lightfast runs, the more it understands about your team, your patterns, and your stage. 27 28 ## Intelligence 29 30 Intelligence will compose memory into action. Skills, workflows, rules, and permissions — defined once, enforced continuously. 31 32 The target: express an invariant — *"every production release needs an approved review and a closed ticket"* — and the layer enforces it at all times, not on a schedule. 33 34 This layer is in development. Today, Lightfast exposes memory through cited answers and a proxy primitive (below) that executes provider actions on behalf of agents. 35 36 ## Agents 37 38 Agents operate on memory through the REST API, TypeScript SDK, and MCP server — the same primitives available to people. 39 40 The `proxy` primitive is the first step toward intent-based resolution: `proxy.search` discovers connected providers, resources, and available actions; `proxy.call` executes an action (`github.list-pull-requests`) through Lightfast with auth handled. Action-level today; higher-order intent resolution — *find the owner of this system, open a PR with full context* — is where this is headed. 41 42 The destination: as the number of agents in your company grows, one system they all operate through — with shared memory, shared context, and your rules enforced at the kernel level. 43 44 Surfaces today: REST API, TypeScript SDK, MCP (Claude, Cursor, Codex). IDE and CI surfaces are planned. 45 46 ## The Loop 47 48 Where Lightfast is going — bug → fix → changelog, without anyone copy-pasting: 49 50 1. **Signal lands.** A Sentry error and a customer report resolve to the same symptom. 51 2. **Context stitches.** Lightfast correlates the symptom to a commit, PRs, owner, and prior discussion. 52 3. **Fix ships.** A PR opens with full context, merges, deploys. 53 4. **Loop closes.** A changelog entry, a customer reply, and a docs update auto-draft — each human-reviewable. 54 55 Today, steps 1 and 2 are addressable through memory + Explore. Steps 3 and 4 — the write-back and auto-drafting — are the roadmap. Every feature Lightfast ships is meant to make your team smarter, not just the product larger. 56 57 ## Supported Sources 58 59 **Available now:** 60 61 | Source | Events | 62 |--------|--------| 63 | **GitHub** | Push, pull requests, issues, code reviews | 64 | **Vercel** | Deployments, project activity | 65 | **Sentry** | Errors, issues, alerts | 66 | **Linear** | Issues, comments, projects, cycles | 67 68 **Coming soon:** 69 70 - Dev & ops: Slack, Notion, Confluence, PagerDuty 71 - Business: Stripe, HubSpot, Apollo 72 - [Request an integration →](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/issues) 73 74 ## Integrate in 2 Ways 75 76 ### 1. TypeScript SDK 77 78 ```bash 79 npm install lightfast 80 ``` 81 82 ```typescript 83 import { createLightfast } from "lightfast"; 84 85 const lf = createLightfast(process.env.LIGHTFAST_API_KEY!); 86 87 // Semantic search across all connected sources 88 const { results } = await lf.search({ 89 query: "deployment errors last week", 90 limit: 10, 91 }); 92 93 // Discover connected providers, resources, and available actions 94 const { connections } = await lf.proxy.search(); 95 96 // Execute a provider action through Lightfast (auth handled) 97 const { status, data } = await lf.proxy.call({ 98 action: "github.list-pull-requests", 99 params: { owner: "acme", repo: "web" }, 100 }); 101 ``` 102 103 ### 2. MCP Server (Claude, Cursor, Codex) 104 105 Connect AI assistants directly to your workspace via [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/). 106 107
108 Claude Desktop 109 110 Add to `claude_desktop_config.json`: 111 112 ```json 113 { 114 "mcpServers": { 115 "lightfast": { 116 "command": "npx", 117 "args": ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"], 118 "env": { 119 "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY": "sk-lf-..." 120 } 121 } 122 } 123 } 124 ``` 125 126
127 128
129 Claude Code (CLI) 130 131 Add to `.mcp.json` in your project root (or `~/.claude.json` for global): 132 133 ```json 134 { 135 "mcpServers": { 136 "lightfast": { 137 "type": "stdio", 138 "command": "npx", 139 "args": ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"], 140 "env": { 141 "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY": "sk-lf-..." 142 } 143 } 144 } 145 } 146 ``` 147 148 Or use the CLI: `claude mcp add lightfast --scope project -- npx -y @lightfastai/mcp` 149 150
151 152
153 Cursor 154 155 Add to `.cursor/mcp.json` in your project root: 156 157 ```json 158 { 159 "mcpServers": { 160 "lightfast": { 161 "command": "npx", 162 "args": ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"], 163 "env": { 164 "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY": "sk-lf-..." 165 } 166 } 167 } 168 } 169 ``` 170 171
172 173
174 OpenAI Codex 175 176 Add to `~/.codex/config.toml`: 177 178 ```toml 179 [mcp_servers.lightfast] 180 command = "npx" 181 args = ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"] 182 183 [mcp_servers.lightfast.env] 184 LIGHTFAST_API_KEY = "sk-lf-..." 185 ``` 186 187
188 189 **Available tools:** 190 - `lightfast_search` — semantic search across connected sources, with cited results 191 - `lightfast_proxy_search` — discover connected providers, resources, and available provider actions 192 - `lightfast_proxy_call` — execute a provider action through Lightfast (auth handled) 193 194 ## Security 195 196 - **Your data stays yours** — We never train on your data. Complete tenant isolation. 197 - **Encrypted at rest and in transit** — Industry-standard security practices 198 - **Role-based access** — Workspace permissions mirror your source permissions 199 - **Self-hosted option** — Coming soon for enterprises with strict data residency requirements 200 201 ## Requirements 202 203 - Node.js >= 18 204 - A Lightfast API key — [get started](https://lightfast.ai) 205 206 ## Documentation 207 208 - [API Reference](https://lightfast.ai/docs/api-reference/getting-started/overview) — Full endpoint documentation 209 - [SDK Guide](https://lightfast.ai/docs/integrate/sdk) — TypeScript SDK usage 210 - [MCP Setup](https://lightfast.ai/docs/integrate/mcp) — Configure AI assistants 211 - [Changelog](https://lightfast.ai/changelog) — Latest updates and releases 212 - [Blog](https://lightfast.ai/blog) — Tutorials, announcements, and deep dives 213 214 ## Contributing 215 216 We welcome contributions! See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines. 217 218 ```bash 219 git clone https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast.git 220 cd lightfast 221 pnpm install 222 pnpm dev 223 ``` 224 225 ## Community 226 227 - [Discord](https://discord.gg/YqPDfcar2C) — Chat with the team and community 228 - [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/issues) — Report bugs and request features 229 - [Twitter](https://x.com/lightfastai) — Follow for updates 230 231 ## License 232 233 Lightfast is licensed under the [Functional Source License, Version 1.1 (FSL-1.1-ALv2)](LICENSE) — a source-available license that automatically converts to Apache 2.0 after 2 years. 234 235 **For Users**: You can use Lightfast freely for internal business purposes, education, and research. Each version becomes fully open source (Apache 2.0) two years after release. 236 237 See [LICENSING.md](LICENSING.md) for complete details. 238

The destination: as the number of agents in your company grows, one system they all operate through — with shared memory, shared context, and your rules enforced at the kernel level. Surfaces today: REST API, TypeScript SDK, MCP (Claude, Cursor, Codex). IDE and CI surfaces are planned. ## The Loop Where Lightfast is going — bug → fix → changelog, without anyone copy-pasting: 1. **Signal lands.** A Sentry error and a customer report resolve to the same symptom. 2. **Context stitches.** Lightfast correlates the symptom to a commit, PRs, owner, and prior discussion. 3. **Fix ships.** A PR opens with full context, merges, deploys. 4. **Loop closes.** A changelog entry, a customer reply, and a docs update auto-draft — each human-reviewable. Today, steps 1 and 2 are addressable through memory + Explore. Steps 3 and 4 — the write-back and auto-drafting — are the roadmap. Every feature Lightfast ships is meant to make your team smarter, not just the product larger. ## Supported Sources **Available now:** | Source | Events | |--------|--------| | **GitHub** | Push, pull requests, issues, code reviews | | **Vercel** | Deployments, project activity | | **Sentry** | Errors, issues, alerts | | **Linear** | Issues, comments, projects, cycles | **Coming soon:** - Dev & ops: Slack, Notion, Confluence, PagerDuty - Business: Stripe, HubSpot, Apollo - [Request an integration →](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/issues) ## Integrate in 2 Ways ### 1. TypeScript SDK ```bash npm install lightfast ``` ```typescript import { createLightfast } from "lightfast"; const lf = createLightfast(process.env.LIGHTFAST_API_KEY!); // Semantic search across all connected sources const { results } = await lf.search({ query: "deployment errors last week", limit: 10, }); // Discover connected providers, resources, and available actions const { connections } = await lf.proxy.search(); // Execute a provider action through Lightfast (auth handled) const { status, data } = await lf.proxy.call({ action: "github.list-pull-requests", params: { owner: "acme", repo: "web" }, }); ``` ### 2. MCP Server (Claude, Cursor, Codex) Connect AI assistants directly to your workspace via [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/).
Claude Desktop Add to `claude_desktop_config.json`: ```json { "mcpServers": { "lightfast": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"], "env": { "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY": "sk-lf-..." } } } } ```
Claude Code (CLI) Add to `.mcp.json` in your project root (or `~/.claude.json` for global): ```json { "mcpServers": { "lightfast": { "type": "stdio", "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"], "env": { "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY": "sk-lf-..." } } } } ``` Or use the CLI: `claude mcp add lightfast --scope project -- npx -y @lightfastai/mcp`
Cursor Add to `.cursor/mcp.json` in your project root: ```json { "mcpServers": { "lightfast": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"], "env": { "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY": "sk-lf-..." } } } } ```
OpenAI Codex Add to `~/.codex/config.toml`: ```toml [mcp_servers.lightfast] command = "npx" args = ["-y", "@lightfastai/mcp"] [mcp_servers.lightfast.env] LIGHTFAST_API_KEY = "sk-lf-..." ```
**Available tools:** - `lightfast_search` — semantic search across connected sources, with cited results - `lightfast_proxy_search` — discover connected providers, resources, and available provider actions - `lightfast_proxy_call` — execute a provider action through Lightfast (auth handled) ## Security - **Your data stays yours** — We never train on your data. Complete tenant isolation. - **Encrypted at rest and in transit** — Industry-standard security practices - **Role-based access** — Workspace permissions mirror your source permissions - **Self-hosted option** — Coming soon for enterprises with strict data residency requirements ## Requirements - Node.js >= 18 - A Lightfast API key — [get started](https://lightfast.ai) ## Documentation - [API Reference](https://lightfast.ai/docs/api-reference/getting-started/overview) — Full endpoint documentation - [SDK Guide](https://lightfast.ai/docs/integrate/sdk) — TypeScript SDK usage - [MCP Setup](https://lightfast.ai/docs/integrate/mcp) — Configure AI assistants - [Changelog](https://lightfast.ai/changelog) — Latest updates and releases - [Blog](https://lightfast.ai/blog) — Tutorials, announcements, and deep dives ## Contributing We welcome contributions! See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines. ```bash git clone https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast.git cd lightfast pnpm install pnpm dev ``` ## Community - [Discord](https://discord.gg/YqPDfcar2C) — Chat with the team and community - [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/issues) — Report bugs and request features - [Twitter](https://x.com/lightfastai) — Follow for updates ## License Lightfast is licensed under the [Functional Source License, Version 1.1 (FSL-1.1-ALv2)](LICENSE) — a source-available license that automatically converts to Apache 2.0 after 2 years. **For Users**: You can use Lightfast freely for internal business purposes, education, and research. Each version becomes fully open source (Apache 2.0) two years after release. See [LICENSING.md](LICENSING.md) for complete details.

9	---
10	
11	## Mission
12	
13	Be the operating layer for every team — so agents and people can observe, reason, and act across the entire tool stack through a single system.
14	
15	## Vision
16	
17	Any agent or engineer can understand what is happening, why it happened, and what should happen next — across every tool, every team, every decision — without knowing which tools exist or how they work.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 1. Problem Statement
22	
23	Lightfast is an operating layer that sits between agents and apps. The layer ingests events from connected tools, stores them as a cited temporal graph, resolves intent into action against the correct tool, and enforces team-defined invariants continuously.
24	
25	The layer addresses four operational problems:
26	
27	- Tool memory is siloed. Events live only in the tool that produced them, so no system correlates causality, ownership, or relationships across the stack.
28	- Agents target tools directly. Every new agent means N×M integrations, drifting API usage, and no shared context across runs.
29	- Team rules are enforced by convention. Release checks, review gates, and policy rules live in documents, not in a kernel that applies them at all times.
30	- Answers cannot cite their source. Summaries without evidence cannot be trusted when humans or agents act on them.
31	
32	Important boundary:
33	
34	- Lightfast is an operating layer. It mediates between tools; it does not replace them.
35	- Lightfast is not a data warehouse, a BI tool, or a general-purpose observability platform.
36	- A successful operation ends at a cited answer or a resolved action against a connected tool, not at raw data delivery.
37	
38	## 2. Goals and Non-Goals
39	
40	### 2.1 Goals
41	
42	- Observe every event across every connected tool automatically and continuously.
43	- Store events as immutable, causally ordered facts with semantic embeddings in a temporal graph.
44	- Cite every answer, action, and decision back to source events.
45	- Resolve agent intent into action against the correct connected tool without per-agent integration work.
46	- Enforce team-defined invariants continuously, not on a schedule.
47	- Expose the same primitives to agents and to people (REST API, SDK, MCP, webhooks).
48	- Preserve complete tenant isolation. Customer data is never used for model training.
49	
50	### 2.2 Non-Goals
51	
52	- Replacing existing tools in the stack. Teams keep using what they use.
53	- General-purpose analytics, business intelligence, or data warehousing.
54	- Black-box decisions. Every action traces to source events; no summary ships without verification.
55	- Rebuilding a unified UI for every connected tool.
56	- Agent orchestration as a product surface. Agents operate through Lightfast; Lightfast does not orchestrate agents.
57	
58	## 3. System Overview
59	
60	### 3.1 Abstraction Layers
61	
62	Lightfast is a pipeline: **Observe → Remember → Reason → Act.** Each layer is a distinct concern.
63	
64	1. `Observe`
65	   - Ingests events from connected tools via webhooks, polling, or push APIs.
66	   - Normalizes platform payloads into a stable event model.
67	
68	2. `Remember`
69	   - Stores events as immutable facts with causal ordering.
70	   - Resolves entities across tools (commits, issues, deployments, incidents, people).
71	   - Maintains a temporal graph and semantic embeddings over those entities.
72	
73	3. `Reason`
74	   - Executes skills, workflows, and rules against memory.
75	   - Detects patterns, predicts outcomes, and enforces invariants.
76	   - Produces cited answers to queries from agents and people.
77	
78	4. `Act`
79	   - Resolves intent into write operations against the correct connected tool.
80	   - Performs side effects (PR comments, issue updates, messages, deploys) with provenance attached.
81	
82	### 3.2 Main Components
83	
84	1. `Connection Layer`
85	   - Manages OAuth flows, credential storage, webhook registration, and backfills per tool.
86	   - Further specification deferred.
87	
88	2. `Event Store`
89	   - Append-only store of normalized events with causal ordering.
90	   - Further specification deferred.
91	
92	3. `Graph and Embeddings`
93	   - Temporal graph of entities and relationships with semantic embeddings.
94	   - Further specification deferred.
95	
96	4. `Reasoning Runtime`
97	   - Executes skills, workflows, and rules against memory.
98	   - Further specification deferred.
99	
100	5. `Action Resolver`
101	   - Translates intent into write operations against connected tools.
102	   - Further specification deferred.
103	
104	6. `Access Surfaces`
105	   - REST API, TypeScript SDK, MCP server, webhooks.
106	   - Further specification deferred.
107	
108	### 3.3 External Dependencies
109	
110	- Connected tools (for example GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel). The set is extensible.
111	- Identity provider for tenant authentication.
112	- Vector index and graph storage.
113	- Background job runtime for ingestion and long-running workflows.
114	
115	## 4. Core Domain Model
116	
117	Entity field specifications are deferred to a future revision. This section lists the primary entity types and their present state.
118	
119	Status tags:
120	- `(shipped v0.1.0)` — entity exists in the running system and is covered by the public API.
121	- `(partial v0.1.0)` — entity exists in limited form; the role defined here is broader than what ships.
122	- `(planned)` — entity is part of the designed system but has no implementation yet.
123	
124	### 4.1 Entities
125	
126	#### 4.1.1 Event `(shipped v0.1.0)`
127	
128	An immutable fact emitted by a connected tool and normalized into the Lightfast event model. Once recorded, an event is never modified.
129	
130	#### 4.1.2 Entity `(shipped v0.1.0)`
131	
132	A resolved object in the temporal graph — for example a commit, issue, deployment, incident, or person — synthesized from one or more events.
133	
134	#### 4.1.3 Connection `(shipped v0.1.0)`
135	
136	A binding between a tenant and a connected tool, including credentials, scopes, and lifecycle state.
137	
138	#### 4.1.4 Skill `(planned)`
139	
140	A composable capability that reads memory and optionally invokes the Action Resolver.
141	
142	#### 4.1.5 Workflow `(planned)`
143	
144	An ordered composition of skills, triggered by events or schedules.
145	
146	#### 4.1.6 Rule `(planned)`
147	
148	A team-defined invariant continuously enforced against memory.
149	
150	#### 4.1.7 Agent `(partial v0.1.0)`
151	
152	A principal — human or automated — that interacts with Lightfast through an access surface. In `v0.1.0` an agent is any caller authenticated by an API key or session token; the expanded role (identity, permissions scoped per skill and rule) is planned.
153	
154	## 5. Principles
155	
156	- **Primitives over features.** Every capability is a configuration of the same building blocks, not a new subsystem.
157	- **Events are facts.** Immutable, causally ordered. Interpretations layer on top. History is never rewritten.
158	- **Intent over API calls.** Agents express what they want. The system resolves how.
159	- **Cite everything.** Every answer, every action, every decision traces back to source events.
160	- **Privacy by default.** Complete tenant isolation. Customer data is never used for training.
161	
162	## 6. Measures
163	
164	- **Coverage.** Fraction of a team's tool stack observed and connected.
165	- **Precision.** Accuracy of cited answers against ground-truth source events.
166	- **Latency.** Time from event ingestion to availability in memory, and from query to cited answer.
167	- **Trust.** Fraction of answers and actions with verifiable source evidence and causal rationale.
168	- **Adoption.** Time-to-integrate per tool and density of agent activity routed through the layer.
169	
170	---
171	
172	## Deferred Sections
173	
174	The following sections are intentionally omitted at this draft level and will be populated in future revisions:
175	
176	- Detailed field definitions for each entity in Section 4.
177	- Event lifecycle and state machines.
178	- Consistency, ordering, and idempotency guarantees.
179	- Authorization model and scope primitives.
180	- Error classes and recovery behavior.
181	- Observability contract (logs, metrics, traces).
182	- Conformance and compatibility rules.
